events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need produce JSON answer. Need interpret scenario: pipe bedding & backfill, lifts too thick for compactor, rough-complete. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Need apply project specs and IPC 306. Need know typical lift thickness for pipe bedding/backfill compaction. IPC? Maybe "IPC 306" could be a project spec/standard. Need infer. In industrial sites, backfill lifts typically 6-8 inches loose, max 12 in depending on compactor. Lifts too thick for compactor means exceeding spec, likely >12 in or >8 in. Need decide fail, risk high/medium? Pipe bedding & backfill compaction critical for support and load distribution; too thick lifts result in inadequate compaction, voids, settlement, pipe damage. Decision fail. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 70-85%. Value numeric reading: estimate lift thickness e.g., 18 inches (0.45 m) vs allowed 8 inches (200 mm). Need include value as numeric reading/computed quantity. Could set value 18 (inches). Confidence 0.8. Findings: backfill lifts placed in excessive thickness preventing adequate compaction; likely low density/non-uniform support. Actions: stop backfill, remove/rework lifts to specified max thickness, recompact in lifts per spec, proof roll/density test. Rationale: IPC 306 and project specs limit lift thickness to ensure specified compaction; observed lifts thicker than compactor capability, resulting in non-compliant compaction and risk of settlement/pipe failure. References: project specs, IPC 306. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

The observed lifts are estimated at about 18 in., which exceeds the typical/equipment-limited allowable lift thickness (commonly 6–8 in. loose for small compactors, or as specified). This results in non-compliant compaction and a defective installation that threatens long-term pipe support.",
